Name: Bulbasaur
Flavor-Text: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.

Name: Ivysaur
Effects: Take 2 [G] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The more sunlight Ivysaur bathes in, the more strength wells up within it, allowing the bud on its back to grow larger.

Name: Venusaur
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
A bewitching aroma wafts from its flower.
The fragrance becalms those engaged in a battle.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Spinarak
Flavor-Text: Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 more.
Flavor-Text:
It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey.
When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

Name: Sunkern
Flavor-Text:
It is very weak.
Its only means of defense is to shake its leaves desperately at its attacker.

Name: Sunflora
Effects: If Quick-Grow Extract is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

Name: Burmy
Flavor-Text: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

Name: Mothim
Flavor-Text: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

Name: Charmander
Flavor-Text:
From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail.
Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may take a [R] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to your Active [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Charizard
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet.
It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

Name: Mega Charizard ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Houndour
Effects:
Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench.
(Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndoom
Flavor-Text: Upon hearing its eerie howls, other Pokémon get the shivers and head straight back to their nests.

Name: Squirtle
Flavor-Text: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.

Name: Wartortle
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.
Flavor-Text: It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to maintain its balance while swimming.

Name: Blastoise
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack also does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The rocket cannons on its shell fire jets of water capable of punching holes through thick steel.

Name: Mega Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Basculin
Effects: If this Pokémon moved from your Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
Anglers love the fight this Pokémon puts up on the hook.
And there are always more to catch— many people release them into lakes illicitly.

Name: Clauncher
Flavor-Text:
Clauncher’s claws will regrow if they fall off.
The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn’t appeal to all tastes.

Name: Clawitzer
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.

Name: Magnemite
Flavor-Text: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

Name: Magneton
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Three Magnemite are linked by a strong magnetic force.
Earaches will occur if you get too close.

Name: Magnezone
Effects:
During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to use an attack, your opponent flips a coin.
If tails, that attack doesn't happen.
Flavor-Text: Exposure to a special magnetic field changed Magneton’s molecular structure, turning it into Magnezone.

Name: Emolga
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries.
Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.

Name: Helioptile
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text:
The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation.
Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

Name: Heliolisk
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Put a random Supporter card from your deck into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It loves to bite and yank people’s hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Move all [P] Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Its cries sound like incantations to torment the foe.
It appears where you least expect it.

Name: Solosis
Flavor-Text: Many say that the special liquid covering this Pokémon’s body would allow it to survive in the vacuum of space.

Name: Duosion
Flavor-Text:
Its brain has split into two, and the two halves rarely think alike.
Its actions are utterly unpredictable.

Name: Reuniclus
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +30 HP for each [P] Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text:
It’s said that drinking the liquid surrounding Reuniclus grants wisdom.
Problem is, the liquid is highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.

Name: Spritzee
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it.
Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee.

Name: Aromatisse
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon cost 1 [C] more.
Flavor-Text: The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: This attack's damage is reduced by the amount of damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

Name: Onix
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage from—and effects of—attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

Name: Makuhita
Flavor-Text:
It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over.
It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

Name: Hariyama
Flavor-Text:
It stomps on the ground to build power.
It can send a 10-ton truck flying with a straight-arm punch.

Name: Nosepass
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The magnet in Nosepass’s nose provides an unerring compass, making it an excellent partner for Trainers going on a journey.

Name: Mega Lopunny ex
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mienfoo
Flavor-Text:
Though small, Mienfoo’s temperament is fierce.
Any creature that approaches Mienfoo carelessly will be greeted with a flurry of graceful attacks.

Name: Mienshao
Flavor-Text: Delivered at blinding speeds, kicks from this Pokémon can shatter massive boulders into tiny pieces.

Name: Grimer
Flavor-Text:
Appears in filthy areas.
It thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

Name: Muk
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: They love to gather in smelly areas where sludge accumulates, making the stench around them worse.

Name: Purrloin
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

Name: Liepard
Effects:
Your opponent reveals a random card from their hand and shuffles it into their deck.
Shuffle this Pokémon into your deck.
Flavor-Text:
Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure.
This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text:
Its favorite places are unsanitary ones.
If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Garbodor
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body.
The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.

Name: Steelix
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It is thought its body transformed as a result of iron accumulating internally from swallowing soil.

Name: Mega Steelix ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks and has no Weakness.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Take 2 [M] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
It exudes strong magnetism from all over.
It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.

Name: Genesect
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
This ancient bug Pokémon was altered by Team Plasma.
They upgraded the cannon on its back.

Name: Ditto
Effects:
Choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon's attacks, except any Pokémon ex, and use it as this attack.
If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It can freely recombine its own cellular structure to transform into other life-forms.

Name: Porygon
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text:
State-of-the-art technology was used to create Porygon.
It was the first artificial Pokémon to be created via computer programming.

Name: Porygon2
Flavor-Text:
This is a Porygon that was updated with special data.
Porygon2 develops itself by learning about many different subjects all on its own.

Name: Porygon-Z
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Trainer card in your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: Porygon-Z had a program installed to allow it to move between dimensions, but the program also caused instability in Porygon-Z’s behavior.

Name: Starly
Effects: Put 1 random Starly from your deck onto your Bench.
Flavor-Text:
Because they are weak individually, they form groups.
However, they bicker if the group grows too big.

Name: Staravia
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They maintain huge flocks, although fierce scuffles break out between various flocks.

Name: Staraptor
Effects:
Flip 4 coins.
This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
It never stops attacking even if it is injured.
It fusses over the shape of its comb.

Name: Buneary
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them.
This attack becomes stronger with training.

Name: Lopunny
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Once hot seasons are over, Lopunny’s coat will be replaced with fur that holds a lot of insulating air in preparation for colder weather.

Name: Bouffalant
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals.
Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Clemont's Backpack
Description: During this turn, attacks used by your Magneton or Heliolisk do +20 damage to your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Quick-Grow Extract
Description: Choose 1 of your [G] Pokémon in play. Put a random [G] Pokémon from your deck that evolves from that Pokémon onto that Pokémon to evolve it. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.

Name: Clemont
Description: Put 2 random cards from among Magneton, Heliolisk, and Clemont's Backpack from your deck into your hand.

Name: Serena
Description: Put a random Mega Evolution Pokémon ex from your deck into your hand.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 1 more.
Flavor-Text:
It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey.
When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

Name: Sunflora
Effects: If Quick-Grow Extract is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Since it converts sunlight into energy, it is always looking in the direction of the sun.

Name: Reuniclus
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon gets +30 HP for each [P] Energy attached to it.
Flavor-Text:
It’s said that drinking the liquid surrounding Reuniclus grants wisdom.
Problem is, the liquid is highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: This attack's damage is reduced by the amount of damage this Pokémon has on it.
Flavor-Text: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

Name: Trubbish
Flavor-Text:
Its favorite places are unsanitary ones.
If you leave trash lying around, you could even find one of these Pokémon living in your room.

Name: Buneary
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text:
Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them.
This attack becomes stronger with training.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Charizard ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Lopunny ex
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Steelix ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks and has no Weakness.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Clemont
Description: Put 2 random cards from among Magneton, Heliolisk, and Clemont's Backpack from your deck into your hand.

Name: Serena
Description: Put a random Mega Evolution Pokémon ex from your deck into your hand.

Name: Mega Venusaur ex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Asleep.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Blastoise ex
Effects: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra [W] Energy attached, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Lopunny ex
Effects:
Flip 2 coins.
This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Steelix ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks and has no Weakness.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Mega Charizard ex
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Oddish
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text:
If exposed to moonlight, it starts to move.
It roams far and wide at night to scatter its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Flavor-Text:
Its pistils exude an incredibly foul odor.
The horrid stench can cause fainting at a distance of 1.25 miles.

Name: Vileplume
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text:
It has the world’s largest petals.
With every step, the petals shake out heavy clouds of toxic pollen.

Name: Charizard
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text:
Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet.
It blows out fire at very high temperatures.

Name: Shellder
Flavor-Text:
It is encased in a shell that is harder than diamond.
Inside, however, it is surprisingly tender.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: Cloyster that live in seas with harsh tidal currents grow large, sharp spikes on their shells.

Name: Sandshrew
Flavor-Text:
It burrows into the ground to create its nest.
If hard stones impede its tunneling, it uses its sharp claws to shatter them and then carries on digging.

Name: Sandslash
Flavor-Text:
It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws.
Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.

Name: Type: Null
Effects:
Flip a coin.
If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific mission, it went berserk during an experiment, so it was cryogenically frozen.

Name: Silvally
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.
Flavor-Text:
This is its form once it has awakened and evolved.
Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

Name: Buzzwole ex
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Big Beat.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Lunala ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may move all [P] Energy from 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Guzzlord ex
Effects:
Flip a coin until you get tails.
For each heads, discard a random Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Solgaleo ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Aegislash
Ability-Effect: Attacks used by your [P] Pokémon and [M] Pokémon do +30 damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text:
Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land.
His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.

Name: Quick-Grow Extract
Description: Choose 1 of your [G] Pokémon in play. Put a random [G] Pokémon from your deck that evolves from that Pokémon onto that Pokémon to evolve it. You can't use this card during your first turn or on a Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
